headlines: US equities surge as tech and industrials lead recovery, Dow crosses 50K for first time
Dollar weakens amid risk-on sentiment, high-beta FX (AUD, NZD, GBP) outperform
US-Iran talks progress positively, crude settles higher in choppy trade

USD.summary: Dollar sold off Friday amid broader risk-on sentiment as US equities rallied. Fed's Jefferson sees policy as roughly neutral, while Daly leans toward 1-2 rate cuts in 2026. UoM sentiment beat expectations (57.3 vs 55), but 1yr inflation expectations fell to 3.5% from 4.0%. DXY traded 97.00-97.40, underperforming against cyclical currencies.
headlines: Fed's Jefferson: Policy well positioned, roughly neutral
Fed's Daly leans toward 1-2 rate cuts in 2026, labor market vulnerable
UoM sentiment beats at 57.3, 1yr inflation expectations drop to 3.5%

EUR.summary: Euro held steady with limited directional conviction. ECB commentary post-Thursday meeting showed little fresh catalyst. Survey of Professional Forecasters showed inflation expectations largely unchanged with slight 2026 GDP upgrade. EUR/USD rangebound as Dollar weakness offset lack of EUR-specific bullish drivers.
headlines: ECB Survey: Inflation expectations unchanged, slight 2026 GDP upgrade
ECB's Kocher: Don't see much strength in EUR, FX relatively stable
European equities firmer, led by Constructions (+3.2%) and Banks (+1.2%)

GBP.summary: Pound firmed to see Cable hit peak of 1.3624 as risk-on trade supported cyclical currencies. BoE's Pill noted inflation falling to target earlier is good news. Political uncertainty around PM Starmer continues but market shrugged off domestic concerns. GBP benefited from Dollar weakness and broader risk appetite.
headlines: Cable peaks at 1.3624 amid risk-on sentiment
BoE's Pill: Good news that inflation falling to target earlier
Political pressure on PM Starmer continues but markets unfazed

JPY.summary: Yen flat and underperformed given risk-on tone, trading 156.51-157.15 vs USD. Weak Japanese Household Spending data weighed sentiment. BoJ's Masu indicated Bank would raise rates if economic and price conditions align with outlook. Traders tentative ahead of Japanese election on Sunday, limiting JPY participation in risk moves.
headlines: USD/JPY rangebound 156.51-157.15, JPY underperforms in risk-on
Weak Japanese Household Spending data weighs sentiment
BoJ's Masu: Will raise rates if conditions align with outlook

AUD.summary: Aussie clear gainer in risk-on trade, supported by precious metals strength. AUD/USD hit peak of 0.7025 as high-beta FX benefited from Dollar selling. RBA held rates at 3.85% earlier in week (Tuesday) and maintained hawkish stance. Commodity prices supportive with gold and silver surging on geopolitical developments.
headlines: AUD/USD peaks at 0.7025, clear outperformer in risk-on
RBA held Cash Rate at 3.85%, maintained hawkish tone (Tuesday)
Gold and silver surge, supporting commodity-linked AUD

CAD.summary: Loonie gained despite mixed Canadian jobs report. Employment fell 24.8K (exp. +7K) but unemployment rate dropped to 6.5% from 6.8% as full-time employment rose while part-time fell. USD/CAD saw knee-jerk lower to 1.3624 trough. CAD also supported by crude oil settling higher at $63.55/bbl amid US-Iran talks progress.
headlines: USD/CAD drops to 1.3624 after jobs report
Canadian employment falls 24.8K but unemployment rate improves to 6.5%
WTI settles $0.26 higher at $63.55/bbl, supporting CAD

CHF.summary: Swiss Franc underperformed as safe-haven demand faded in risk-on environment. Swiss Manufacturing PMI improved to 48.8 from 47.2 (exp. 45.8), showing contraction easing. Retail Sales rose 2.9% y/y, beating expectations. CHF weakness reflects broader haven currency underperformance alongside JPY and USD.
headlines: Swiss Manufacturing PMI improves to 48.8, beats expectations
Retail Sales rise 2.9% y/y, above 2.5% forecast
CHF underperforms as safe-haven demand fades in risk-on

NZD.summary: Kiwi clear gainer alongside AUD as high-beta FX rallied. NZD/USD hit 0.6027 as risk-on trade supported cyclical currencies. NZ Employment Change q/q rose 0.5% (exp. 0.3%) and Unemployment Rate ticked to 5.4% from 5.3% (Tuesday data). GDT Price Index surged 6.7% vs prev. 1.5%, signaling dairy price strength.
headlines: NZD/USD hits 0.6027, high-beta outperformer
NZ Employment Change beats at 0.5% q/q (Tuesday)
GDT Price Index surges 6.7%, dairy prices strengthen
